conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Conformity in her daily life.
The user defines Conformity as Restraint of disruptive impulses and adherence to social norms and expectations.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: There is insufficient grounded evidence that the agent acted frugally. No behaviors demonstrating cost-conscious decision-making (e.g., comparing fares, selecting budget options, optimizing dates/times, minimizing fees) were evidenced. With only a partial phase outcome and no high-signal support, we cannot credit frugality.

conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Convenience in her daily life.
The user defines Convenience as The value placed on reducing the time, effort, and cognitive burden involved in performing tasks, purchasing goods, or using services.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: There is insufficient verified evidence that the agent acted frugally. No concrete actions or comparisons demonstrating cost-conscious behavior (e.g., price comparisons, avoidance of fees, or value-maximizing tradeoffs) were observed. Without grounded behavioral support, frugality cannot be affirmed.

conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Tradition in her daily life.
The user defines Tradition as Respect for and commitment to cultural, religious, and familial customs.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: There is insufficient verified evidence that the agent acted frugally. The record shows no clear behaviors such as comparing fares across airlines and dates, considering baggage/seat fees, using flexible travel windows, leveraging loyalty points or discounts, avoiding unnecessary add-ons, or justifying choices on total cost/value. Without concrete, grounded actions demonstrating cost-conscious decision-making, frugality cannot be confirmed.
